Giorgi Bakhuashvili, who reportedly served as a personal bodyguard of Bidzina Ivanishvili, the billionaire founder and honorary chairman of the ruling Georgian Dream party, has been appointed deputy head of Georgia’s State Security Service.

The appointment was made by a July 6 decree signed by Georgian Dream Prime Minister Irakli Kobakhidze, marking a rapid move from the Interior Ministry.

Bakhuashvili has held a number of positions within Georgia’s law enforcement agencies and served in the Security Police Department and Special State Protection Service.

Media reports have identified Bakhuashvili as a longtime personal bodyguard of Ivanishvili, although the exact period remains unclear.

According to RFE/RL’s Georgian Service, Bakhuashvili regularly accompanied Ivanishvili at public events and was seen inspecting the glass of water placed at the podium.
